name: Cuban Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in the lukewarm water.
2. Add the salt, sugar, and cornmeal to the yeast mixture. Stir well.
3. Gradually add the sifted flour to the mixture, stirring until a stiff dough forms.
4. Shape the dough into a ball and place it in a greased bowl.
5. Cover the bowl with a towel and let it rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface.
7. Shape the dough into long, French-style loaves or round, Italian-style loaves.
8. Sprinkle the baking sheet liberally with cornmeal.
9. Place the shaped loaves on the prepared baking sheet.
10. Allow the loaves to rise for 5 minutes.
11. Slash the tops of the loaves in two or three places using a knife.
12. Brush the loaves lightly with water.
13. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
14. Place a pan of boiling water on the bottom shelf of the oven.
15. Bake the breads for 40-45 minutes, until they are crusty and golden brown.
James Beard
